- Goal model: Collect colored blocks in the order shown at bottom (pink→red→green→blue). Pink is already the player. Next target is Red.
- Plan: Move player UP to align with Red block (rows 19-22), then extend path RIGHT to connect.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Player at rows 30-35
- Path extends to col 46
- Green block at rows 31-34, cols 42-45 (collected, now part of path)
- Red block at rows 31-34, cols 36-39 (collected, part of path)
- Blue block at rows 25-28, cols 42-45 (still on the wall, not collected)
The green block was collected! Now I need to collect the blue block. The blue block is at rows 25-28, and the player is at rows 30-35. I need to move UP to align with the blue block.
Player rows 30-35, blue at rows 25-28. Need to move up about 5 rows. One UP moves 6 rows, so one UP would move player to rows 24-29, which overlaps with blue at 25-28.
Let me move UP once, then RIGHT to extend the path to the blue block.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green collected! Now need blue.
# Player at rows 30-35, blue at rows 25-28
# Move UP 1 to align (30-35 → 24-29, overlaps blue at 25-28)
# Then RIGHT to extend path to blue

action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
